column1: Assumptions locked below table. All dollar values in USD. Revenue/unit = blended $27.50 (70% clip bundles at $24.99 / 30% rails at $34.99).
Labor rate: $15.00/hr (owner opportunity cost). Machine: Bambu P1S at $699, 5000-hr depreciation life = $0.14/hr. PLA+ at $12/kg bulk (10kg+).
Shipping: USPS Ground Advantage via Pirate Ship; $4.50 blended avg (8% surcharge in effect Apr 26 2026 – Jan 17 2027). Platform: Etsy 6.5% txn + 3.0% payment = $2.37/order at $24.99 avg.

column3: Annual units

column17: Machine depreciation dominates at low utilization: printer runs ~45 min/week at $0.14/hr = $0.105 allocated to 1 unit. Effective depreciation $4.20/unit vs $0.10 at 20/week. Labor high because packaging overhead (90 sec) amortized over single unit. Not a viable steady state — a test phase or slow launch scenario only.

Bulk filament savings begin at 3-spool orders (~$14/kg instead of $17). Machine depreciation collapses: printer runs ~3.5 hr/week × $0.14 = $0.49 ÷ 5 units = $0.098 rounded to $0.84 (includes 20% efficiency overhead for turnaround time). Labor improves with batching: 2–3 plates per session reduces per-unit prep time. Break-even on startup capital within Month 1.

TARGET OPERATING RANGE. PLA+ at $12/kg bulk (10kg bundles). Machine depreciation nearly negligible: 14hr/week × $0.14 = $1.96 ÷ 20 = $0.098. Labor: harvest + QC (8 sec/unit = $0.033) + packaging (90 sec/order at 3-clip avg = $0.375/unit) + daily shipping batch (10 min ÷ 20 units = $0.125). Total labor $0.533 rounded to $0.62 with overhead. Business plan 72-73% margin target is achievable at $29+ AOV (rail mix pushes it up).

Near single-printer ceiling (35+ hr print/week). Marginal material savings from 10kg bulk (now at tier pricing). Labor stable — post-processing 1.5 hr/week solo. Consider second printer at this point for redundancy and capacity buffer. Monthly gross ~$5,913 revenue; $4,170 gross profit.

2nd printer acquired (adds ~$0.035/unit to depreciation). Contractor post-processing introduced at 10 hrs/week × $15 = $600/month → $0.14/unit labor addition. Labor line rises vs 50/week: contractor overhead absorbed. Platform fees stable. Monthly revenue ~$11,825; gross profit $8,278. 2nd printer payback: 2–3 weeks at this volume.

column3: AOV ($)

column10: Single $12–15 clips with $4 shipping. Shipping = 27% of revenue. Platform fees eat another 9.5%. No bundling = no margin. This scenario is unsustainable. Bundle enforcement is the #1 operational priority.

column1: Realistic — bundle discipline + rail mix (model basis)
column10: Assumes 70% 3-clip bundles at $24.99 + 30% rails at $34.99. Standard production assumption throughout main table.

column1: Optimistic — premium PETG + homelab kit bundles
column10: PETG at $16/kg adds $0.40/unit material. Higher AOV absorbs it. Premium positioning unlocks if heat-resistance messaging lands. Requires validated customer willingness-to-pay (confirm by Month 3-4).

$22/kg would be reached if Chinese tariffs escalate significantly further. Push Plastic (US-made PLA+) at $20/kg is the tariff hedge; domestic sourcing costs more but eliminates tariff exposure. $342/month margin hit at 20/week — material still only 4% of revenue.

If the 8% surcharge extends past Jan 2027 (historically USPS extensions are common). $0.50 increase per order. Moderate impact — $195/month reduction at 20/week. Mitigated by encouraging local pickup / digital delivery bundles where applicable.

column3: Capital required ($)

column2: 30–40 units/week sustained for 2 consecutive weeks; single printer at 80%+ utilization
column6: Primary trigger is demand, not readiness. Do not buy 2nd printer speculatively. Wait for order backlog evidence.

column2: Post-processing + packaging exceeds 2 hours/day AND volume is 75+ units/week
column4: Net-positive immediately if enables +20 units/week throughput
column5: Revenue gained from freed-up operator time > contractor cost at this volume
column6: 1099 contractor only — no W-2. Avoids payroll overhead (FICA, workers comp). Requires IRS 1099-NEC at year end if paid $600+.

column2: Monthly filament spend exceeds $100 (consuming 8kg+/month = 40+ units/week of clips)
column3: $120–160 per 10kg spool at $12–16/kg
column4: 3–4 months in savings vs per-spool pricing
column5: Primarily a cash flow optimization — saves $1–3/kg vs single spools

column3: $524 (Anycubic 50kg at $10.49/kg)
column5: Storage: 50kg of PLA+ requires 50 sealed containers with desiccant; humidity control critical. Only worthwhile above 125 units/week.

column2: Etsy volume reliably 50+ units/week AND listing exposure plateauing OR diversification needed
column3: $300–500 FBA prep + inventory build
column4: Incremental — FBA revenue net of higher fees (28–32% vs Etsy 9.5%)
column5: FBA eliminates per-order packaging/label labor but imposes higher fee load. Net margin lower but volume potential much higher. Reduces Etsy revenue concentration risk.

column2: 2-printer fleet at 80%+ utilization consistently for 2+ weeks; demand exceeds 75 units/week
column6: Expected timeline: Month 8–14 if growth trajectory holds. Not needed in Year 1 for most launches.
